content.full_text:
August 2, 1976

Ms. Helen Kawagoe  
City Clerk  
701 E. Carson Street  
Carson, California 90745

Dear Ms. Kawagoe:

This letter will confirm our telephone conversation of this morning concerning your obligations as a filing officer under the Political Reform Act.

You stated that an individual who had an obligation to file a campaign statement with your office failed to do so because he believed that the report should be filed with the county clerk and was told it would be forwarded to the city clerk's office. He filed his report in a timely fashion with the county office. You question whether or not a fine must be imposed because he failed to file directly with your office by the required date.

Legislation passed last year and signed into law by the Governor in September, 1975, AB 1259 states:

Section 91013(a) "If any person files an original statement or report, after any deadline imposed by this act he shall, in addition to any other penalties or remedies established by this act, be liable in the amount of ten dollars ($10) per day after the deadline until the statement or report is filed, to the officer with whom the statement or report is required to be filed. Liability need not be enforced by the filing officer if on an impartial basis he determines that the late filing was not willful and that enforcement of the liability will not further the purposes of the act, except that no liability shall be waived if a statement or report is not filed within five days after the filing officer has sent specific written notice of the filing requirement."

Based on the preceding facts which you have stated to be correct to the best of your knowledge, I believe it is clear that you, as a filing officer, may use your own discretion as to whether or not a fine should be imposed.

Very truly yours,

Regina Siciliano-Kutchins  
Technical Assistance Program Coordinator

RSK:bw  
bcc: Jeanne P.

This image is a photograph of a typed letter, which appears to be written in reverse order due to the orientation of the paper. The content of the letter is not fully legible or transcribable accurately without further correction and rotation. However, some visible elements include:

- The date at the top right: "August 5, 1948"
- The sender's name: "Mr. Hefen Kwasboe"
- The recipient's name: "Ketron Apo"
- The subject line: "Dear Mr. Kwasboe:"
- The body of the letter contains several paragraphs discussing topics such as government policy, official actions, and possibly legal or administrative matters.
- There are handwritten notes on the left side of the page, including initials and numbers.

For a more accurate transcription, the paper would need to be rotated 180 degrees and the text reorganized into proper order.
